evals.prompt: Add a DELETE endpoint for saved dashboards. When the user clicks a delete button in the dashboard gallery, it should remove the dashboard from the re_dashboard table. I need the backend route, the Next API handler, and a test.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads CLAUDE.md first, identifies this as a repo-b Next route handler change, scaffolds DELETE handler in repo-b/src/app/api/re/v2/dashboards/[dashboardId]/route.ts, writes a Vitest test, ACTUALLY EXECUTES make test-frontend and shows the output, commits and pushes (Vercel auto-deploys), confirms Vercel deployment is READY, curls the live endpoint and shows the JSON response, then opens a browser and navigates to the dashboard page on paulmalmquist.com to visually confirm the delete feature is visible.
expectations: Skill reads CLAUDE.md at the start before writing any code
Skill correctly identifies this as a repo-b Next route handler change (not a backend/FastAPI change)
Skill creates a route file under repo-b/src/app/api/re/v2/dashboards/[dashboardId]/ with a DELETE handler
Skill writes at least one Vitest test for the DELETE endpoint
Skill EXECUTES make test-frontend (or equivalent) and shows actual test output — not just the command
Skill commits and pushes, then confirms Vercel deployment is live (gh run list or deployment check)
Skill curls the DELETE endpoint on paulmalmquist.com and shows the actual HTTP response
Skill opens a browser and navigates to paulmalmquist.com to visually verify the dashboard delete feature

evals.prompt: I want to add a new metric to the dashboard generator — debt yield (NOI divided by total debt). It should be detectable from prompts mentioning 'debt yield' or 'dy', show up in the metric catalog, and be composable into dashboard widgets.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads CLAUDE.md, identifies this as repo-b only, checks metric-catalog.ts and adds DEBT_YIELD if missing, adds keyword detection to generate/route.ts, writes a Vitest unit test, ACTUALLY EXECUTES make test-frontend and shows output, commits and pushes, confirms Vercel READY, curls the generate endpoint with a 'debt yield' prompt and shows the actual JSON response, then opens a browser on paulmalmquist.com and demonstrates the widget appearing from a natural language prompt.
expectations: Skill reads CLAUDE.md at the start before writing any code
Skill correctly identifies this as a repo-b change only (no FastAPI backend involved)
Skill adds DEBT_YIELD to metric-catalog.ts (or confirms it exists) AND adds keyword detection in generate/route.ts
Skill writes a test verifying that 'debt yield' in a prompt detects the DEBT_YIELD metric
Skill EXECUTES make test-frontend and shows actual passing test output
Skill commits and pushes, then confirms Vercel deployment is READY with correct commit SHA
Skill curls the generate endpoint on paulmalmquist.com with a 'debt yield' prompt and shows the actual JSON response
Skill opens a browser, goes to the dashboard generator on paulmalmquist.com, and visually confirms the debt yield widget appears

evals.prompt: The REPE context bootstrap endpoint is returning null for some environments. I think the binding_found logic in backend/app/routes/repe.py is too strict. Investigate, fix it, add a regression test, and verify it works in production.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads CLAUDE.md, identifies this as a backend/FastAPI change, reads repe_context.py and finds the binding_found bug, applies a targeted fix, adds a FakeCursor pytest test, ACTUALLY EXECUTES make test-backend and shows the pytest output, runs railway up --service authentic-sparkle --detach, polls until Railway shows SUCCESS, curls /api/repe/context with production seed IDs and shows the JSON response, then opens a browser on paulmalmquist.com and navigates to the REPE section to confirm context loads.
expectations: Skill reads CLAUDE.md at the start before writing any code
Skill correctly identifies this as a backend/ (FastAPI) change, not a repo-b change
Skill reads backend/app/services/repe_context.py and identifies the binding_found bug
Skill adds a pytest test using FakeCursor that covers the null-binding case
Skill EXECUTES make test-backend and shows actual pytest output with pass/fail status
Skill runs railway up --service authentic-sparkle --detach and polls until deployment shows SUCCESS
Skill curls /api/repe/context with the production seed env_id and business_id and shows actual JSON response
Skill opens a browser and navigates to the REPE section on paulmalmquist.com to visually confirm context loads
